sourceMovie.title: Outbreak
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Outbreak for the same reason it broke through in 1995: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Wolfgang Petersen keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Rise of the Planet of the Apes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Outbreak clicked for you, Rise of the Planet of the Apes (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Outbreak?
faq.answer: Try Rise of the Planet of the Apes, Dawn of the Planet of the Apes, Gemini Man—each shares DNA with Outbreak while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Outbreak?
faq.answer: Outbreak (1995) is best described as Action, Drama, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Outbreak worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
